# mercado-livre-bulk-upload

> CLI and publishing pipeline for Mercado Livre bulk product bulk uploads. Provides a Typer-based CLI (`ml-upload`), spreadsheet parsing and normalization, category/attribute resolution, image and clip upload, encrypted token storage, and machine-readable reporting. Target Python version: >=3.13. Development tooling: Black, Ruff, MyPy, PyTest.

Primary package: `mercadolivre_upload` — the CLI entrypoint is `ml-upload -> mercadolivre_upload.main:main` and the composition root for publishing flows is `mercadolivre_upload/cli/commands/upload.py`.

Start here: read the README first for high-level workflow, then inspect `mercadolivre_upload/cli/app.py` and `mercadolivre_upload/cli/commands/upload.py` to see how commands are wired; consult `mercadolivre_upload/application/publish_product.py` for publish orchestration and `mercadolivre_upload/application/ports.py` for interfaces.

## Documentation

- [README.md](README.md): Primary project overview, quick start, CLI examples (validate/upload), token/security guidance, and developer commands.
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): Agent-focused operational guidance and developer shortcuts for automated agents.
- [skills/](skills/): Domain SKILL.md files — reusable agent knowledge per workflow area. Read the relevant SKILL.md before touching domain code (e.g. `skills/fiscal-upload/SKILL.md` before touching `domain/fiscal/`).
- [.github/copilot/copilot-instructions.md](.github/copilot/copilot-instructions.md): Copilot instructions blueprint generated for this repository (coding conventions and examples).

## Specifications

- [pyproject.toml](pyproject.toml): Project metadata, dependencies, Black/Ruff/mypy/pytest configuration; authoritative toolchain settings.
- [config/standard_fields.yaml](config/standard_fields.yaml): Runtime mapping defaults and explicit field mappings used by the upload flow.
- [config/shipping.yaml](config/shipping.yaml): Shipping policy toggles and defaults consumed by ShippingResolver.
- [config/attribute_rules.yaml](config/attribute_rules.yaml): Attribute classification and sanitization rules.

## Examples & Fixtures

- [anuncios/](anuncios/): Example images and fixtures used by validation and tests.
- [anuncios/2.xlsx](anuncios/2.xlsx): Example spreadsheet referenced in docs and tests (fixture for validation examples).

## Code (key entry points)

- [main.py](main.py): Top-level compatibility entrypoint that runs the package CLI.
- [mercadolivre_upload/cli/app.py](mercadolivre_upload/cli/app.py): Typer app, console theme, and top-level command registration.
- [mercadolivre_upload/cli/commands/upload.py](mercadolivre_upload/cli/commands/upload.py): Composition root for upload/validate flows; builds PublishProductUseCase and wires adapters.
- [mercadolivre_upload/application/publish_product.py](mercadolivre_upload/application/publish_product.py): Publish orchestration and use-case logic (PublishProductUseCase).
- [mercadolivre_upload/application/ports.py](mercadolivre_upload/application/ports.py): Protocols and port definitions (interfaces used by use-cases).
- [mercadolivre_upload/infrastructure/http.py](mercadolivre_upload/infrastructure/http.py): Resilient HTTP client (retry/backoff/rate limiting) used by MLApiClient.
- [mercadolivre_upload/adapters/image_uploader.py](mercadolivre_upload/adapters/image_uploader.py): Image uploader adapter used by the publish flow.
- [mercadolivre_upload/api/client.py](mercadolivre_upload/api/client.py): Mercado Livre API client implementation.

## Tests & QA

- [tests/test_cli.py](tests/test_cli.py): CLI tests demonstrating Typer/CliRunner usage and mocking patterns.
- [tests/](tests/): Full test suite; run with `uv run pytest -q`. Tests use `runner.isolated_filesystem()` and `unittest.mock.patch` extensively.

## Configuration & Scripts

- [requirements.txt](requirements.txt): Runtime dependencies.
- [pyproject.toml](pyproject.toml): Dev dependencies, tooling, and pytest coverage gating (`--cov-fail-under=60`).
- [uv.lock](uv.lock): (environment lock file used with `uv` wrapper).

## Runtime & Data

- `cache/`: runtime caches and generated reports (attribute/prediction caches under `cache/categories/`).
- `cache/reports/`: default location for validation/upload summary reports and failed-items exports.

## Security note

This repository stores tokens in an encrypted file by default (`tokens.json.enc`) and reads secrets from environment variables (`ML_PIPE_*`). Do NOT commit plaintext tokens or secret values.

## Where to begin for programmatic analysis

Priority order for LLMs and tooling:
1. README.md — quick start and high-level behavior
2. mercadolivre_upload/cli/app.py and mercadolivre_upload/cli/commands/upload.py — CLI wiring and composition
3. mercadolivre_upload/application/publish_product.py and application/ports.py — core use-case orchestration and interfaces
4. mercadolivre_upload/infrastructure/http.py and mercadolivre_upload/api/client.py — API/resilience patterns
5. tests/ and anuncios/ fixtures — concrete examples and expected behaviors

## Optional (helpful context)

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) contains agent-specific workflows and testing tips for automated code agents.
- [.github/](.github/): CI and workflow configuration.

--

This file is intended to be a concise, prioritized navigation map for LLMs and humans to understand the repository structure, where to find authoritative specifications, and which files to read first for each task.
